notes: Model routing for Claude runtimes lives in 'tiers'; routing for Codex runtimes / codex-exec cross-dispatch lives in 'codex_tiers' (see references/model-routing-codex.md). Other runtimes (Cursor, Gemini, ...) ignore both and map the abstract tiers onto their own lineup — see 'Portability & Model Routing' in SKILL.md.
limits.max_concurrent_agents caps SIMULTANEOUS dispatches, not the total. A large PR may select 12 agents; launch them in waves of <= max_concurrent_agents and queue the rest.
fallback: null means: on refusal/failure, record the dimension as 'no coverage — degraded' in the report and continue. Never abort the run.

cheap.notes: Triage, patch application, mechanical work. NEVER attach an effort value — Haiku 4.5 rejects the effort parameter.

standard.notes: Most review dimensions. Effort low/medium/high only — never xhigh (costs more than Opus at comparable accuracy; escalate to 'strong' instead). Ships cyber safeguards, so it CAN refuse — same fallback handling as 'deep'.

strong.notes: Coordinator, adjudication, and universal fallback. No meaningful classifier-refusal risk in normal PR review — the safe choice for anything that must not stall.

deep.notes: Risk-surface review only. Safety classifiers can refuse security-adjacent content (elevated false positives on auth/vulns/injection). Mandatory 30-day retention, no ZDR. Expensive.

codex_tiers.notes: Always pass -m and model_reasoning_effort explicitly (never inherit ~/.codex/config.toml defaults). Review leaves run '-s read-only'. Fallback/pin model: gpt-5.5. Verify IDs against ~/.codex/models_cache.json after any model-generation bump.

cheap.notes: Triage, mechanical checks, patch application. 'medium' for larger shards. Cheapest tier; 'ultra' accepted but may clamp to max.

standard.notes: Most review dimensions; effort up to 'high'. GPT-5.5-class capability at ~half cost.

strong.notes: Adjudication-grade review and universal fallback; effort up to 'xhigh'. Also the vision tier (attach images with -i) — GPT vision outperforms Claude Opus 4.8 on screenshot/UI review.

deep.notes: Risk-surface review. 'ultra' (parallel delegate subagents) is opt-in only for plan-level passes — never an automatic escalation.
